chunk_summary: A one-page prescription template for chronic kidney and liver disease patients, adapted for India, includes food swaps, lab-driven restrictions, and reassessment guidelines for clinic and ward implementation.
learning_objectives: Understand how to create a one-page prescription template for chronic kidney and liver disease patients.
Learn to incorporate India-specific food swaps for realistic dietary adherence.
Identify key lab parameters and anthropometric measures for nutritional planning.
Develop strategies for reassessment and escalation based on clinical trends.
key_facts: Reassess nutritional plans every 2–4 weeks using height velocity and MUAC trends.
Target intake: kcal/day or kcal/kg/day and g protein/kg/day, with oral densification or enteral support if intake <80% for 2 weeks.
Key labs for CKD: sodium, potassium (K+), bicarbonate/total CO2, phosphate, calcium, and PTH; for liver disease: bilirubin, INR, and albumin.
Avoid cola-type drinks, seasoning packets, potassium chloride substitutes, and packaged snacks with phosphate/potassium additives.
School tiffin template: controlled-salt home item (e.g., poha/upma/idli), protein item (e.g., egg/paneer/curd), low-additive snack (e.g., roasted makhana), and water.
reference_quote: ‘Translate restrictions into additive rules rather than food bans: no cola-type drinks, no seasoning packets, no “lowsalt” potassium chloride substitutes, and avoid packaged snacks with phosphate/potassium additives.’ (Source: https://www.kidney.org/sites/default/files/docs/cpgpednutr2008.pdf)
document_key_topics: India-adapted food swaps for CKD and liver disease
